title: Auth (Magic Link + Google OAuth)
deliverable: User can sign in via Supabase magic link or Google OAuth from a 'Cihazlar arası eşitle' affordance in the header / dedicated 'auth' view. Session persists across reloads. App remains fully functional and data-loss-free without signing in.

in: Supabase Auth client initialisation (already in lib/supabase.js from Block 16; confirm it exists)
'Cihazlar arası eşitle' button in the header that either opens the auth view or shows user avatar/sign-out when authed
Auth view state 'auth' added to the view router in App.jsx
AuthView component: email input + 'Sihirli bağlantı gönder' button (magic link) AND 'Google ile devam et' button
onAuthStateChange listener in App.jsx that sets session + user state on load and on every auth event

Session state: { session, user } held in AuthContext (src/context/AuthContext.jsx) and exposed via useAuth hook; consumed by any component via the hook — NOT prop-drilled from App.jsx. The view state remains plain App.jsx useState (prop-based) (C4)
Signed-in header state: small avatar/initials chip + 'Çıkış Yap' affordance
Magic-link post-send confirmation message shown inline (Turkish copy)
OAuth redirect handling: Supabase client picks up the URL hash/code on return from Google; no custom redirect page needed for SPA

Supabase dashboard configuration documentation: enable Google provider, enable email magic-link, add redirect URL for production domain only (niyet.burakgizlice.com); *.pages.dev wildcard registration deferred until production is live (C5)
AuthContext defined here (src/context/AuthContext.jsx) so Block 18 and any other block can import useAuth to access user + session without prop chains (C4)
Profile row is auto-created by the handle_new_user DB trigger defined in Block 16 — no manual upsert by Block 17 or Block 18 (C3)
Loading/initialising state (spinner or skeleton) while Supabase session is being restored on mount
Error handling: display Turkish error message if magic-link send fails or OAuth errors out
Sign-out clears session state in App.jsx (localStorage data remains untouched)
out: Data merge/sync on login — Block 18 owns this entirely
profiles row upsert — handled automatically by the Block 16 handle_new_user DB trigger; neither Block 17 nor Block 18 should upsert it (C3)
Password-based auth — explicitly excluded by PRD
Supabase schema or RLS changes — Block 16 owns schema
Any change to queue/chains/done logic — those blocks own their data

files.purpose: Confirm supabaseClient is exported; ensure createClient is called with VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. No logic changes needed if Block 16 created it correctly — verify only.
Auth / Sync screen: email input + magic-link send button + Google OAuth button + post-send confirmation message + back navigation. Turkish copy throughout. Emerald dark theme, gold accents.

Renders the auth affordance in the header. When logged out: 'Cihazlar arası eşitle' button (cloud-sync icon + text). When logged in: small avatar chip (user initials or Google photo) + 'Çıkış Yap' link. Receives session/user props and onOpenAuth / onSignOut callbacks.

Add session/user state (useState). Add useEffect with supabase.auth.getSession() on mount and supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange() listener. Add 'auth' to view router. Pass session/user/onOpenAuth/onSignOut to Header and AuthView. Sign-out calls supabase.auth.signOut().

AuthProvider component and AuthContext. Owns session/user/loading state, the onAuthStateChange listener, and all auth actions (signInWithMagicLink, signInWithGoogle, signOut). Wraps the app in main.jsx. Defines the useAuth hook exported from this file (C4).
Re-exports useAuth from src/context/AuthContext.jsx for convenient import paths. Keeps auth concerns out of App render body. Block 18 and other consumers import useAuth directly (C4).
Exports REDIRECT_URL constant (hardcoded to 'https://niyet.burakgizlice.com' at launch per C5). Single place to update when *.pages.dev preview auth is later enabled.
Developer runbook: step-by-step Supabase dashboard config — enable Google provider (client ID + secret), enable email magic-link, add redirect URLs. Not shipped to users; kept in repo for onboarding.

implementation_steps.description: Verify lib/supabase.js exports a named supabaseClient
implementation_steps.details: Check that Block 16 created src/lib/supabase.js with: import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'; export const supabaseClient = createClient(import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL, import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY). If the export name differs, update all imports to be consistent before adding auth logic.

export const REDIRECT_URL = 'https://niyet.burakgizlice.com'; At launch only the production domain is registered as an allowed redirect URL in Supabase. Preview-URL auth (*.pages.dev) is deferred until production is live (C5). For local development, manually add localhost to the Supabase allowlist — do not use window.location.origin to avoid accidentally allowing untrusted preview URLs before they are registered.

Hook body: (1) useState for session (null), user (null), loading (true). (2) useEffect on mount: call supabaseClient.auth.getSession() → set session + user + loading=false. Subscribe to supabaseClient.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, session) => { setSession(session); setUser(session?.user ?? null); }) — return unsubscribe. (3) signInWithMagicLink(email): calls supabaseClient.auth.signInWithOtp({ email, options: { emailRedirectTo: REDIRECT_URL } }). Returns { error }. (4) signInWithGoogle(): calls supabaseClient.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google', options: { redirectTo: REDIRECT_URL } }). (5) signOut(): supabaseClient.auth.signOut() then clear local session state. Export { session, user, loading, signInWithMagicLink, signInWithGoogle, signOut }.

Wrap the app in <AuthProvider> (from src/context/AuthContext.jsx) in main.jsx or at the top of App.jsx. App.jsx itself reads { loading } from useAuth() only to decide whether to render the loading screen — it does NOT hold session/user state. While loading is true, render a full-screen loading state (emerald background, Arabic نية wordmark, subtle pulse — no spinner jank). Add 'auth' to the view discriminant union type comment. Render <AuthView> when view === 'auth'. The view state (useState for 'main'|'auth'|...) stays local to App.jsx and is passed as props (C4).

Layout (max-width 480px, centred, emerald dark): (1) Back button top-left ('← Geri'). (2) Heading: 'Cihazlar arası eşitle'. (3) Subtext: 'Telefonunda başladığın adımları bilgisayarında devam ettir.' (4) Divider. (5) Email input (type=email, placeholder='e-posta adresin', onKeyDown: Enter triggers send). (6) Primary gold button: 'Sihirli bağlantı gönder'. On click: call signInWithMagicLink(email); if no error show confirmation message: 'Bağlantı gönderildi — e-postanı kontrol et. Bu sekmeyi açık tut.' in green; if error show error in red. (7) Or-divider. (8) Secondary button: 'Google ile devam et' (Google G icon + text). On click: call signInWithGoogle(). Google button is full-width with white background + dark text (standard Google brand requirement). (9) Footer note (small, muted): 'Şifre yok. Hesap oluşturmak gerekmez.' State: email string, sent boolean, error string.

Receives { session, user, onOpenAuth, onSignOut }. If !session: render a small button — cloud icon + 'Eşitle' text (compact for header). On click: onOpenAuth(). If session: render a pill containing user initials (from user.email first char uppercased) or user_metadata.avatar_url as 24px circle image if available, plus a dropdown or inline 'Çıkış Yap' link. Keep the signed-in state subtle — do not let it dominate the header. Style: emerald dark background, gold text/border on hover.

implementation_steps.description: Integrate AuthHeader into the main Header area
implementation_steps.details: In App.jsx (or the existing Header/Queue component), place <AuthHeader> in the top-right of the header row. AuthHeader reads session/user internally via useAuth — no prop drilling from App.jsx. Pass only onOpenAuth={() => setView('auth')}; signOut is called inside AuthHeader via useAuth (C4). The rest of the header (fire streak, show-count toggle) must not shift layout when auth state changes — use position:absolute or fixed width for the auth affordance slot.

implementation_steps.description: Handle OAuth return / magic-link click deep-link
implementation_steps.details: Supabase JS v2 auto-detects the #access_token fragment or ?code= param in the URL on page load and calls exchangeCodeForSession internally. The onAuthStateChange listener (step 17.3) will fire with event SIGNED_IN automatically. No custom /auth/callback route is needed for a pure SPA. After session is set, clear the URL hash with history.replaceState(null, '', window.location.pathname) to avoid token leakage in browser history.

signOut() in useAuth calls supabaseClient.auth.signOut(). onAuthStateChange fires SIGNED_OUT → session=null, user=null. localStorage queue/chains/done data is NOT cleared — it remains the working state. View stays on whatever was current (no redirect to auth). The 'Cihazlar arası eşitle' affordance reappears in the header.

Create docs/supabase-auth-setup.md. Contents: (1) Authentication → Providers → Email: enable 'Magic Link', disable 'Confirm email' toggle if desired for frictionless dev. (2) Authentication → Providers → Google: enable, paste OAuth 2.0 Client ID and Client Secret from Google Cloud Console. Google Cloud Console setup: create OAuth client, Authorised redirect URIs must include https://<supabase-project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback. (3) Authentication → URL Configuration → Site URL: https://niyet.burakgizlice.com. (4) Redirect URLs at launch: https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/** only. Do NOT add *.pages.dev wildcard yet — preview-URL auth is deferred until production is live (C5). Add *.pages.dev when preview auth support is prioritised. (5) Note: Supabase anon key is safe to commit; RLS enforces row-level isolation per Block 16.

implementation_steps.description: Add profile-row creation hook comment in App.jsx
implementation_steps.details: In AuthContext.jsx's onAuthStateChange handler, after session is set, add a clearly labelled comment: // TODO Block 18: on SIGNED_IN event, call sync.mergeOnLogin(user.id) to merge local data. Note: the profiles row is already created automatically by the handle_new_user DB trigger defined in Block 16 — Block 17 and Block 18 do NOT need to upsert it (C3). This comment serves as the handoff contract to Block 18 for data merge only.

Manually trace: user has queue items in localStorage, opens auth view, signs in via magic link. After SIGNED_IN fires: session is set, user is set, view can return to 'main', queue items from localStorage are still present (Block 18 not yet run). Confirm no code in this block clears or overwrites localStorage keys used by queue/chains/done.

data_structures.name: AuthState
data_structures.shape: { session: Session | null, user: User | null, loading: boolean }
data_structures.notes: Session and User are Supabase types from @supabase/supabase-js. loading is true only during the initial getSession() call on mount. Exported interface for any block that reads auth state.

data_structures.name: AuthActions
data_structures.shape: { signInWithMagicLink: (email: string) => Promise<{ error: AuthError | null }>, signInWithGoogle: () => void, signOut: () => Promise<void> }
data_structures.notes: Returned alongside AuthState from useAuth hook. signInWithGoogle is fire-and-forget (redirect-based); signInWithMagicLink returns error for inline UI feedback.

data_structures.name: AuthViewLocalState
data_structures.shape: { email: string, sent: boolean, error: string | null }
data_structures.notes: Component-local state in AuthView.jsx. 'sent' drives the confirmation message. 'error' drives the error message. Resets when email input changes.

interfaces.name: useAuth
interfaces.signature: () => { session: Session | null, user: User | null, loading: boolean, signInWithMagicLink: (email: string) => Promise<{ error: AuthError | null }>, signInWithGoogle: () => void, signOut: () => Promise<void> }
interfaces.purpose: Hook that reads from AuthContext. AuthContext.jsx (src/context/AuthContext.jsx) is the provider that owns session/user state and the onAuthStateChange listener. Any component or hook — including Block 18 sync logic — imports useAuth directly to access user+session (C4). Auth state is NOT prop-drilled from App.jsx; only the view state remains prop-based.

interfaces.name: AuthHeader props
interfaces.signature: { session: Session | null, user: User | null, onOpenAuth: () => void, onSignOut: () => void }
interfaces.purpose: Presentational header chip that reads session/user from useAuth internally. Receives only onOpenAuth callback from parent (C4). Parent (App.jsx) owns view-routing only.

interfaces.name: AuthView props
interfaces.signature: { onBack: () => void, signInWithMagicLink: (email: string) => Promise<{ error: AuthError | null }>, signInWithGoogle: () => void }
interfaces.purpose: Auth screen receives actions but no session — it does not need to know when sign-in completes (parent's onAuthStateChange handles view transitions if desired).

acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'Cihazlar arası eşitle' in the header opens the 'auth' view
Auth view shows an email input and a 'Sihirli bağlantı gönder' button
Auth view shows a 'Google ile devam et' button
Submitting a valid email shows 'Bağlantı gönderildi — e-postanı kontrol et. Bu sekmeyi açık tut.' confirmation inline
Submitting an invalid email shows a Turkish error message
Clicking the magic link in email returns the user to the app and the session is established
Clicking 'Google ile devam et' redirects to Google OAuth and returns to the app with session established
After sign-in, the header shows user initials/avatar chip and a 'Çıkış Yap' affordance
After sign-in, the 'Cihazlar arası eşitle' button is no longer visible
Clicking 'Çıkış Yap' clears the session; the header returns to the unauthenticated state
All queue items, chains, and done items present before sign-in are still present after sign-in
All queue items, chains, and done items present before sign-out are still present after sign-out
App is fully functional (add steps, complete tasks, load chains) when not signed in
Refreshing the page while signed in restores the session without requiring re-authentication
While session is being restored on mount, a loading state is shown (not a flash of the main view with session=null)
URL hash/token fragment is cleared from the address bar after OAuth/magic-link return
No console errors related to auth on a clean anonymous load
verification: Open app in a private/incognito window — confirm all features work, no auth prompts, no console errors
Click 'Cihazlar arası eşitle' — confirm auth view opens with correct Turkish copy
Enter an email and send magic link — confirm inline confirmation message appears
Add a task to the queue before signing in, then complete magic-link sign-in flow — confirm task is still in the queue
After sign-in, refresh the page — confirm session is restored and header shows user chip
Click 'Çıkış Yap' — confirm header returns to 'Cihazlar arası eşitle', queue data intact
Click 'Google ile devam et' in a browser that has a Google session — confirm redirect and return to app with session
In Supabase dashboard Auth → Users, confirm a new user row was created after sign-in
Check browser localStorage — confirm supabase.auth.token (or equivalent Supabase v2 key) is set after sign-in
Open browser devtools Network tab during magic-link send — confirm POST to Supabase /auth/v1/otp returns 200
Simulate Supabase error (wrong VITE_SUPABASE_URL) — confirm Turkish error message appears in auth view, app does not crash

questionable_decisions.decision: Auth state is held in App.jsx via useAuth hook and passed as props
questionable_decisions.assumption: The component tree is shallow enough (App → Header, Queue, AuthView) that prop drilling is not painful
alternatives: React Context (AuthContext) consumed anywhere in the tree
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Use React Context for auth state — even in a shallow tree it avoids coupling AuthHeader to App's render signature and makes Block 18 cleaner when sync logic needs user.id deep in hooks. Create a minimal AuthContext.jsx alongside useAuth.js.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C4 — locked: AuthContext/useAuth for user+session; view stays prop-based

questionable_decisions.assumption: Both production (niyet.burakgizlice.com) and Cloudflare preview URLs will be registered in Supabase redirect allowlist
alternatives: Hardcode VITE_REDIRECT_URL env var per environment
Use a single canonical production URL always (breaks preview deploys)
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep window.location.origin but add a Cloudflare Pages wildcard *.pages.dev/** in Supabase redirect URL list. Document the exact wildcard syntax as it differs between Supabase versions.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C5 — locked: prod domain only at launch (niyet.burakgizlice.com), *.pages.dev wildcard deferred

questionable_decisions.decision: No post-sign-in view transition — user stays on whatever view was open
questionable_decisions.assumption: The SIGNED_IN event fires and updates the header chip; no navigation is needed
alternatives: Show a 'Hoş geldin' splash for 1s then navigate to main
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Auto-navigate to 'main' after SIGNED_IN, but only if the current view is 'auth'. This avoids stranding the user on the auth screen after a successful sign-in, which would be confusing on the magic-link return.

questionable_decisions.decision: Profile row creation is deferred to Block 18 (noted only as a comment here)
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 18 will always run synchronously after session is established before any Supabase data reads
alternatives: Create the profiles row here on SIGNED_IN using an upsert, before Block 18
Use a Supabase database trigger on auth.users insert to auto-create profiles row
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Use a Supabase database trigger (AFTER INSERT ON auth.users) to auto-create the profiles row with defaults. This removes the race condition entirely and does not require Block 17 or 18 to manage it. Add this to the Block 16 schema spec or Block 18 spec.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C3 — locked: handled by Block 16 DB trigger (handle_new_user), no manual upsert in Block 17 or 18

questionable_decisions.decision: 'Disable email confirmation' is listed as optional in the runbook for frictionless dev
questionable_decisions.assumption: The Supabase magic link OTP flow does not require a separate email confirmation step — the magic link itself is the confirmation
alternatives: Leave email confirmation enabled (users must confirm email before any auth works)
Use signUp flow instead of OTP for first-time users
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Magic link via signInWithOtp does NOT require email confirmation to be disabled — it is self-confirming. Remove the confusing note from the runbook. Leave email confirmation settings at their default.

open_questions: Should the auth view be accessible from anywhere in the app (all views show the header affordance) or only from the main view?
What happens if the user opens the magic link on a different device than the one that requested it — does the session establish on the new device only, or should we warn the user?
Should 'Çıkış Yap' require a confirmation dialog given that the user might accidentally tap it on mobile?
Supabase v2 stores auth state under a localStorage key like 'sb-<ref>-auth-token' — does this conflict with any existing localStorage key naming used in Blocks 1-16?
Is there a desired behaviour if the user is mid-chain (queue has items) and signs in — should the sync (Block 18) run immediately or on next app load?
Google OAuth requires HTTPS — does the local Vite dev server need to be configured with HTTPS, or will Google OAuth testing only happen against preview/production deployments?
Should the 'Cihazlar arası eşitle' affordance show a tooltip or helper text explaining what sync means before the user taps it?
